title: RSA migration: C shims for key lifecycle
description:
Add wolfcrypt_rsa_* C shims to compat_shim.c covering the full RSA key lifecycle. All heap allocation and struct layout lives in C; Rust gets opaque void* handles.

Functions to implement (guarded by #ifndef NO_RSA):
- wolfcrypt_rsa_new() → void*   // malloc + wc_InitRsaKey
- wolfcrypt_rsa_free(void*)
- wolfcrypt_rsa_generate(void*, int bits)  // wc_MakeRsaKey
- wolfcrypt_rsa_import_private_pkcs1(void*, const byte*, word32)  // wc_RsaPrivateKeyDecode (PKCS#1 DER)
- wolfcrypt_rsa_import_public_spki(void*, const byte*, word32)    // wc_GetPubKeyDerToDer or DecodeSubjectPublicKeyInfo + wc_RsaPublicKeyDecode
- wolfcrypt_rsa_export_private_pkcs1(void*, byte*, word32*)       // wc_RsaKeyToDer
- wolfcrypt_rsa_export_public_spki(void*, byte*, word32*)         // wc_RsaKeyToPublicDer (outputs SPKI)
- wolfcrypt_rsa_key_size_bytes(void*) → int                       // wc_RsaEncryptSize

Key format contract:
- Import private: PKCS#1 DER (same as current i2d_RSAPrivateKey / d2i_RSAPrivateKey)
- Import public: SPKI DER (same as current i2d_PUBKEY / d2i_PUBKEY)
- Export private: PKCS#1 DER
- Export public: SPKI DER (via wc_RsaKeyToPublicDer which outputs SubjectPublicKeyInfo)

Each shim returns 0 on success, negative wolfCrypt error code on failure. wolfcrypt_rsa_new returns NULL on error.

Add corresponding extern "C" declarations to wolfcrypt-rs/src/lib.rs under #[cfg(wolfssl_rsa)].

Acceptance criteria:
- All 8 shim functions compile without error in both default and vendored builds
- Manual smoke test: generate key → export PKCS#1 → re-import → export again → bytes match
